  • Procs
        pretty
      • pretty[T: SomeUnit](s: T; precision: int): string

      • enumerateTypesImpl(t: NimNode): NimNode

      • `$`[T: SomeUnit](s: T): string
        to
      • to[T; U](x: T; to: typedesc[U]): U
  • Converters
        toSteradian
      • toSteradian(x: float): Steradian
        toFloat
      • toFloat(x: UnitLess): float
      • toFloat(x: Radian): float
      • toFloat(x: Steradian): float

      • toRadian(x: float): Radian

      • toUnitLess(x: SomeNumber): UnitLess

  • Macros
        <=
      • `<=`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
        /
      • `/`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
        <
      • `<`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
        *
      • `*`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
        -
      • `-`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
        .
      • `.`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: untyped): untyped
        generateSiPrefixedUnits
      • generateSiPrefixedUnits(units: untyped): untyped
        quantityList
      • quantityList(): untyped
        isAUnit
      • isAUnit(x: typed): untyped
        toNaturalUnit
      • toNaturalUnit[T: SomeUnit](t: T): untyped
        defUnit
      • defUnit(arg: untyped): untyped
        ==
      • `==`[T: SomeUnit; U: SomeUnit](x: T; y: U): bool
        +
      • `+`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
  Source   Edit

auto conversion of UnitLess to float is possible so that e.g. sin(5.kg / 10.kg) works as expected!TODO: we should really combine these macros somewhat?

SomeQuantity = BaseQuantity | DerivedQuantity
  Source   Edit 
Second = distinct Time
  Source   Edit 
Meter = distinct Length
  Source   Edit 
Gram = distinct Mass
  Source   Edit 
KiloGram = distinct Gram
KiloGram is special due to being the actual SI unit. Thus defined here   Source   Edit 
Ampere = distinct Current
  Source   Edit 
Kelvin = distinct Temperature
  Source   Edit 
Mol = distinct AmountOfSubstance
  Source   Edit 
Candela = distinct Luminosity
  Source   Edit 
SiUnit = Second | Meter | KiloGram | Ampere | Kelvin | Mol | Candela

Newton = KiloGram•Meter•Second⁻²

Joule = KiloGram•Meter²•Second⁻²

Watt = KiloGram•Meter²•Second⁻³
  Source   Edit 
Ohm = KiloGram•Meter²•Second⁻³•Ampere⁻²
  Source   Edit 
Henry = KiloGram•Meter²•Second⁻²•Ampere⁻²
  Source   Edit 
Farad = Second⁴•Ampere²•Meter⁻²•KiloGram⁻¹
  Source   Edit 
Pascal = KiloGram•Meter⁻¹•Second⁻²
  Source   Edit 
Radian = Meter•Meter⁻¹
  Source   Edit 
Steradian = Meter²•Meter⁻²
  Source   Edit 
Tesla = KiloGram•Ampere⁻¹•Second⁻²
  Source   Edit 
ElectronVolt = distinct Energy
  Source   Edit 
Bar = distinct Pressure
  Source   Edit 
Liter = distinct Length
  Source   Edit 
Degree = distinct Angle
  Source   Edit 
Minute = distinct Time
  Source   Edit 
Hour = distinct Time
  Source   Edit 
Day = distinct Time
  Source   Edit 
Year = distinct Time
  Source   Edit 
Pound = distinct Mass
  Source   Edit 
Inch = distinct Length
  Source   Edit 
Mile = distinct Length
  Source   Edit 
Foot = distinct Length
  Source   Edit 
Yard = distinct Length
  Source   Edit 
Acre = distinct Area
  Source   Edit 
Ounce = distinct Mass
  Source   Edit 
Slug = distinct Mass
  Source   Edit 
PoundForce = distinct Force
  Source   Edit 
Joule•Coulomb⁻¹ = Volt
  Source   Edit 
Ampere•Ohm = Volt
  Source   Edit 
DerivedSiUnits = Newton | Joule
  Source   Edit 
m = Meter
  Source   Edit 
s = Second

  Source   Edit 
g = Gram

° = Degree

  Source   Edit 
day = Day

yr = Year

inch = Inch

mi = Mile

ft = Foot

yd = Yard

acre = Acre
  Source   Edit 
oz = Ounce

slug = Slug

lbf = PoundForce

m•s⁻² = Meter•Second⁻²
  Source   Edit 
g•cm⁻³ = distinct Density
  Source   Edit 
meterPerSecondSquared = Meter•Second⁻²
  Source   Edit 
SiPrefix = enum
  siYocto, siZepto, siAtto, siFemto, siPico, siNano, siMicro, siMilli, siCenti,
  siDeci, siIdentity, siDeca, siHecto, siKilo, siMega, siGiga, siTera, siPeta,
  siExa, siZetta, siYotta
  Source   Edit 
QuantityKind = enum
  qkUnitLess, qkMass, qkLength, qkTime, qkCurrent, qkTemperature,
  qkAmountOfSubstance, qkLuminosity, qkFrequency, qkVelocity, qkAcceleration,
  qkArea, qkMomentum, qkForce, qkEnergy, qkElectricPotential, qkCharge, qkPower,
  qkElectricResistance, qkInductance, qkCapacitance, qkPressure, qkDensity,
  qkAngle, qkSolidAngle, qkMagneticFieldStrength
  Source   Edit 
UnitKind = enum
  ukUnitLess = "UnitLess", ukGram = "Gram", ukMeter = "Meter",
  ukSecond = "Second", ukAmpere = "Ampere", ukKelvin = "Kelvin", ukMol = "Mol",
  ukCandela = "Candela", ukNewton = "Newton", ukJoule = "Joule",
  ukVolt = "Volt", ukHertz = "Hertz", ukCoulomb = "Coulomb", ukWatt = "Watt",
  ukOhm = "Ohm", ukHenry = "Henry", ukFarad = "Farad", ukPascal = "Pascal",
  ukBar = "Bar", ukRadian = "Radian", ukSteradian = "Steradian",
  ukTesla = "Tesla", ukNaturalLength = "NaturalLength",
  ukNaturalMass = "NaturalMass", ukNaturalTime = "NaturalTime",
  ukNaturalEnergy = "NaturalEnergy", ukElectronVolt = "ElectronVolt",
  ukLiter = "Liter", ukDegree = "Degree", ukMinute = "Minute", ukHour = "Hour",
  ukDay = "Day", ukYear = "Year", ukPound = "Pound", ukInch = "Inch",
  ukMile = "Mile", ukFoot = "Foot", ukYard = "Yard", ukOunce = "Ounce",
  ukSlug = "Slug", ukAcre = "Acre", ukPoundForce = "Pound-force" ## Base unit kind stores the fundamental units, which represent the SI units (except for Gram in place of kg)
                                                                 ## that each represent one of the fundamental quantities (the "base quantities" in `QuantityKind`).
                                                                 ## NOTE: The order of `BaseUnitKind`, `UnitKind` and `QuantityKind` is important, both for sorting
                                                                 ## units as well as to allow conversion between the base units / quantities using `ord`.
  Source   Edit 
BaseUnitKind = enum
  buUnitLess = "UnitLess", buGram = "Gram", buMeter = "Meter",
  buSecond = "Second", buAmpere = "Ampere", buKelvin = "Kelvin", buMol = "Mol",
  buCandela = "Candela"
  Source   Edit 
UnitType = enum
  utQuantity, utCompoundQuantity
  Source   Edit 
CTBaseUnit = object
  baseUnit: BaseUnitKind
  power: int
  siPrefix: SiPrefix
  Source   Edit 
CTUnit = object
  name: string
  isShortHand: bool ## stores if the given unit used shorthand `m` or verbose `Meter`
                    ## TODO: can factor be a procedure? Then when generating code, we simply use `getAst` to
                    ## get the body of the conversion and replace `x -> body(x)` inline?
  factor: float
  unitKind: UnitKind
  quantity: QuantityKind
  power: int                 ## each unit needs a power
  siPrefix: SiPrefix         ## and si prefix already! `km²` etc
  case unitType: UnitType
  of utQuantity:
      b: CTBaseUnit          ## TODO: base unit can be takes from a mapping of `UnitKind -> BaseUnitKind`!
    
  of utCompoundQuantity:
      bs: seq[CTBaseUnit]    ## one compound quantity is made up of multiple base units.
  Source   Edit 
CTCompoundUnit = object
  units: seq[CTUnit]
  Source   Edit 
SomeUnit = concept x
    isAUnit(x)
  Source   Edit

Macros

macro quantityList(): untyped
  Source   Edit 
macro isAUnit(x: typed): untyped
  Source   Edit 
macro generateSiPrefixedUnits(units: untyped): untyped
generates all SI prefixed units for all units given. That just means appending each SI prefix to these units, both in long and short form. NOTE: This should only be used on raw units and not explicit compound units!   Source   Edit 
macro defUnit(arg: untyped): untyped
Helper template to define new units (not required to be used manually)   Source   Edit 
macro `==`[T: SomeUnit; U: SomeUnit](x: T; y: U): bool
  Source   Edit 
macro `<`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
  Source   Edit 
macro `<=`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
  Source   Edit 
macro `+`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
  Source   Edit 
macro `-`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
  Source   Edit 
macro `*`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
TODO: can we extract the actual mathy part from x, y instead of using the whole expression? And then reinsert that after our change   Source   Edit 
macro `/`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber; U: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: U): untyped
  Source   Edit 
macro `.`[T: SomeUnit | SomeNumber](x: T; y: untyped): untyped
macro to allow to generate new types on the fly TODO: maybe have an explicit distinction between already defined types and not defined types? Use .! or something like this instead?   Source   Edit 
macro toNaturalUnit[T: SomeUnit](t: T): untyped
parses the unit and converts it to natural units (eV) according to the contained   Source   Edit
